sections.title: Introduction to Future Proche
sections.content: The near future (futur proche) is used to express actions that will happen very soon, in the immediate future. It is formed with the present tense of aller (to go) + the infinitive of the main verb.
examples: Je vais manger (I am going to eat)
Tu vas partir (You are going to leave)
Il va pleuvoir (It is going to rain)

sections.title: Formation
sections.content: Conjugate aller in the present tense and add the infinitive: je vais + infinitive, tu vas + infinitive, il/elle/on va + infinitive, nous allons + infinitive, vous allez + infinitive, ils/elles vont + infinitive.
examples: Je vais parler (I am going to speak)
Tu vas finir (You are going to finish)
Il va vendre (He is going to sell)
Nous allons danser (We are going to dance)
Vous allez choisir (You are going to choose)
Ils vont attendre (They are going to wait)

sections.title: Uses of Future Proche
sections.content: The near future is used for: 1) Actions that will happen very soon, 2) Actions that are about to happen, 3) Intentions or plans for the immediate future, 4) Predictions about the near future.
examples: Je vais sortir dans 5 minutes (I'm going to go out in 5 minutes) - very soon
Attention! Tu vas tomber! (Careful! You're going to fall!) - about to happen
Je vais étudier ce soir (I'm going to study tonight) - intention
Il va neiger demain (It's going to snow tomorrow) - prediction

sections.title: Future Proche vs Future Simple
sections.content: Use futur proche for immediate or near future actions. Use future simple for more distant future actions or for formal/predictive statements.
examples: Je vais manger dans 5 minutes (I'm going to eat in 5 minutes) - near future
Je mangerai demain à cette heure (I will eat tomorrow at this time) - more distant
Il va pleuvoir cet après-midi (It's going to rain this afternoon) - near future
Il pleuvra demain (It will rain tomorrow) - more distant

sections.title: Negation
sections.content: To negate the future proche, place ne/n' before aller and pas after: ne + vais/vas/va/allons/allez/vont + pas + infinitive.
examples: Je ne vais pas manger (I am not going to eat)
Tu ne vas pas partir (You are not going to leave)
Il ne va pas pleuvoir (It is not going to rain)

sections.title: Practice Exercise
sections.content: Write 10 sentences using the near future tense. Describe what you and others are going to do this week.
